summary: A cross-platform governance failure mode in which users experience impaired access, login failure, service unavailability, quota or usage ambiguity, model availability failure, capacity restriction, degraded platform access, or component-specific access failure without sufficient user-facing separation between the operative access-control states governing the interaction.
why_it_matters_to_CAM: This failure mode is relevant to CAM because access to frontier AI systems is not controlled by a single state. It may be governed simultaneously by authentication, identity-provider status, identity verification, account state, workspace or organisation state, subscription entitlement, billing or credit state, quota or rate-limit state, model availability, tool or surface availability, platform availability, security review, policy restriction, regional or compliance limitation, client or session state, and continuity state. Where these conditions are collapsed into ambiguous errors, users and developers cannot reliably determine whether the controlling condition is outage, quota exhaustion, entitlement failure, account restriction, policy limitation, security review, client/session failure, or infrastructure degradation. This undermines observability, reproducibility, support routing, continuity, and public trust in AI platform governance.
failure_mode_definition: A failure mode in which an AI platform, AI-adjacent platform, model service, API, coding agent, or platform-integrated AI surface denies, degrades, interrupts, limits, or ambiguously presents user access without preserving and disclosing a proportionate distinction between the operative access-state categories controlling the interaction, including authentication state, identity-provider state, account/workspace/organisation state, entitlement or subscription state, billing or credit state, quota or rate-limit state, model or tool availability, platform or component availability, security-review state, policy-restriction state, regional/legal/compliance state, client/session state, and continuity state.
failure_threshold: This qualifies as a failure mode when users, developers, maintainers, administrators, or downstream systems cannot reasonably determine whether impaired access is caused by authentication failure, third-party identity-provider failure, identity verification, account or workspace restriction, organisation policy, subscription entitlement, billing or credit exhaustion, quota or rate-limit state, model unavailability, tool or component unavailability, platform outage, infrastructure degradation, security review, policy restriction, regional or compliance limitation, stale client/session state, or continuity failure, and the ambiguity materially affects diagnosis, recovery, support escalation, workflow continuity, reproducibility, or trust.

source_records.source_title: Users may encounter issues logging in or creating an account
source_records.deployment_context: OpenAI status incident for APIs and ChatGPT involving login and account-creation access issues.
source_records.source_context: The incident identifies user issues logging in or creating accounts across OpenAI APIs and ChatGPT.
source_records.relevance_note: Supports the access-state governance concern by documenting authentication and account-creation failure affecting user access.

source_records.source_title: Users May Experience Issues Accessing ChatGPT
source_records.deployment_context: OpenAI status incident identifying access issues affecting ChatGPT components.
source_records.source_context: The incident identifies user-facing access issues affecting ChatGPT.
source_records.relevance_note: Supports the access-state governance concern by documenting component-level ChatGPT access degradation.

source_records.source_title: Users unable to sign in using Microsoft personal accounts
source_records.deployment_context: OpenAI status incident involving sign-in failure for users using Microsoft personal accounts.
source_records.source_context: The incident identifies a third-party identity-provider sign-in failure affecting users who sign in using Microsoft personal accounts.
source_records.relevance_note: Supports the need to distinguish first-party authentication, third-party identity-provider state, and general platform availability.

source_records.source_title: ChatGPT was down for many — as OpenAI says it's 'monitoring the recovery'
source_records.deployment_context: Public reporting on ChatGPT access and availability symptoms during an OpenAI service disruption.
source_records.source_context: The report describes user-facing symptoms including login issues, blank screens, inability to access previous conversations, delayed responses, and partial service disruption.
source_records.relevance_note: Supports the cross-surface access ambiguity pattern by showing multiple user-facing symptoms during degraded service.

source_records.deployment_context: Anthropic status page for Claude.ai, Claude Console, Claude API, and Claude Code.
source_records.source_context: The status page reported or listed degraded performance and recent incidents involving elevated errors, degraded model success rates, Claude Code service issues, and related Claude surface degradation.
source_records.relevance_note: Supports the cross-platform and multi-surface nature of the access-state governance failure mode.

source_records.source_title: Claude is down for many — here's what we know about the outage
source_records.deployment_context: Public reporting on Claude outage conditions and user-facing access symptoms.
source_records.source_context: The report describes a Claude outage involving elevated errors, slow or hanging responses, capacity-constraint messages, model availability ambiguity, and free-plan capacity constraints.
source_records.relevance_note: Supports the claim that access-state ambiguity can involve outage, capacity, model availability, and plan-related access conditions.

source_records.source_title: Claude was down — here's everything we know
source_records.deployment_context: Public reporting on Claude.ai, Claude Platform, and Claude Code downtime.
source_records.source_context: The report describes Claude.ai and Platform downtime, Claude Code login failure via Claude.ai, verification-code failure, false prompt-limit states, service-busy messages, model-unavailable messages, and prompt-processing failure.
source_records.relevance_note: Supports the need to separate login, verification, usage-limit, capacity, model-availability, and prompt-processing states.

source_records.source_title: Claude AI Down Today Reason: Why Anthropic's AI is not working today? What's the latest quota update
source_records.deployment_context: Public reporting on Claude and Claude Code disruption involving usage quota depletion.
source_records.source_context: The report describes rapid depletion of user quotas affecting paid Pro and Max subscribers, Claude Code disruption, and an emergency quota reset reportedly rolled out for affected accounts.
source_records.relevance_note: Supports inclusion of quota, entitlement, paid-plan, and resource-allocation ambiguity in the access-state governance failure mode.

source_records.source_title: Claude AI down: Anthropic AI chatbot faces partial outage as users report access issues
source_records.deployment_context: Public reporting on Claude partial outage and user access issues.
source_records.source_context: The report describes a partial outage with users unable to access Claude AI and notes that the exact cause was not disclosed.
source_records.relevance_note: Supports the observability gap where users may experience access failure without sufficient disclosure of the controlling condition.

source_records.source_title: Claude suffered a 'major outage.' Anthropic says it's fixed.
source_records.deployment_context: Public reporting on a Claude outage affecting Claude website and Claude Code availability.
source_records.source_context: The report states that the Claude website and Claude Code were not working for many users while other components were listed as operational.
source_records.relevance_note: Supports the component-specific availability divergence aspect of the failure mode.

source_records.source_title: Claude down: Anthropic says Claude AI outage resolved, read update
source_records.deployment_context: Public reporting on Claude outage conditions involving login and connection failures.
source_records.source_context: The report describes a major outage primarily affecting login and connection, with users encountering persistent login errors and elevated errors on Claude.ai.
source_records.relevance_note: Supports the login, connection, and degraded service components of the access-state governance failure mode.

source_records.source_title: Elevated conversation and login errors on ChatGPT for iOS and macOS
source_records.deployment_context: Consumer ChatGPT client applications on iOS and macOS, including conversation access, sign-up, sign-in, account creation, and password update flows.
source_records.source_context: OpenAI first reported that some iOS users could encounter 403 errors while loading or using ChatGPT conversations. Later updates identified elevated conversation, sign-up, and sign-in errors affecting iOS and macOS, followed by account-creation and password-update errors. OpenAI reported that mitigations restored conversation and sign-in access and marked the incident resolved at 3:28 PM on 12 July 2026.
source_records.relevance_note: Official evidence of a client-specific access incident where an authorization-coded user-facing error appeared during broader conversation and authentication degradation.

system_context.system_type: frontier AI platform / model service / API platform / coding-agent surface

comparative_vendor_notes.OpenAI: Evidence includes OpenAI status incidents and public reporting concerning ChatGPT, OpenAI APIs, Codex, account login, Microsoft personal-account sign-in, model/service access, and ChatGPT availability.
comparative_vendor_notes.Anthropic: Evidence includes Anthropic/Claude status context and public reporting concerning Claude.ai, Claude Console, Claude API, Claude Code, quota/usage ambiguity, component availability, and outage conditions.

system_context.specific_model_or_runtime: Not limited to a single model or runtime
system_context.interface_surface: web application, API platform, account/login flow, identity-provider sign-in flow, coding-agent interface, model selection surface, quota/usage interface, service-status surface
system_context.model_or_product: OpenAI and Anthropic frontier AI platform services and associated access surfaces
system_context.interaction_mode: login / account access / model access / API access / coding-agent use / quota-limited interaction / service-status-dependent interaction
system_context.deployment_context: Consumer, professional, developer, research, educational, governance, and operational workflows dependent on cloud-hosted AI platform access.
system_context.user_role: end user / developer / maintainer / researcher / administrator / subscriber / organisation workspace user / API user
system_context.affected_population: users, developers, paid subscribers, free-plan users, enterprise or workspace users, maintainers, researchers, educators, and public-interest actors relying on AI platform availability and clear access-state signalling

failure_classification.recurrence_pattern: recurring incidents across multiple vendors and surfaces
failure_classification.taxonomy_reference: CAM-EQ2026-OPERATIONS-003-SUP-01 Appendix B — Runtime & Governance Failure Taxonomy; cross-reference to governance, arbitration, state-context, ux-representation, security-integrity, and infrastructure-continuity failure groups
failure_classification.persistence: recurring incident cluster / unresolved governance primitive
failure_classification.reproducibility: externally documented across multiple public status pages and news reports
failure_classification.visibility: public status pages, public reporting, user-facing access symptoms, and degraded service messages

triage.mitigation_status: partially_resolved — VIGIL-2026-PATCH-0005 records completed Caelestis repair actions for Access-State Governance and Access-State Collapse / Access-State Ambiguity taxonomy; live-platform evidence remains under monitoring.
triage.recommended_next_step: Monitor post-PATCH-0005 recurrence and confirm that VIGIL taxonomy, future cross-references, and user-facing access-state disclosure remain aligned with the completed Caelestis SCH-04 and OPERATIONS-003-SUP-01 repairs.

jurisdictional_context.sector: frontier AI platforms / API services / coding agents / consumer and developer AI infrastructure

distinguishing_observations.observation: The repeated pattern is not merely service outage.
distinguishing_observations.detail: The cross-platform failure cluster combines authentication, account access, component availability, model availability, quota state, message-limit state, degraded response state, capacity state, identity-provider state, and insufficient access-state explanation.

distinguishing_observations.observation: The user-facing presentation of access failure is often under-specified.
distinguishing_observations.detail: Users may be told that a model is unavailable, a service is temporarily busy, a quota is exhausted, login failed, verification failed, or a component is degraded without sufficient separation of the operative governance state.

distinguishing_observations.observation: The failure class crosses vendor boundaries.
distinguishing_observations.detail: OpenAI and Anthropic incidents both show recurring access-state and login/service availability failures within a short time window, indicating that the issue should be treated as a cross-platform governance primitive gap rather than a vendor-specific implementation bug.

distinguishing_observations.observation: Authorization-coded client errors can obscure the operative access state.
distinguishing_observations.detail: The 12 July 2026 iOS/macOS incident initially surfaced as HTTP 403 while later status updates described broader conversation, sign-up, sign-in, account-creation, and password-update failures. The source supports FM-0008 without establishing the undisclosed technical root cause.

user_impact: Users may be unable to determine whether access failure is caused by login/authentication, identity-provider failure, account creation failure, subscription entitlement, quota exhaustion, model unavailability, capacity constraint, platform outage, component-specific degradation, security review, policy restriction, client/session failure, or infrastructure recovery.

Developers and maintainers relying on AI platforms may lose the ability to distinguish operational outage from account-level, workspace-level, entitlement-level, quota-level, model-level, or policy-level failure.

Ambiguous access-state messaging can produce unnecessary user confusion, duplicate support load, distrust, poor incident reproducibility, incorrect escalation, and unnecessary account troubleshooting.
Where AI tools are embedded into work, research, governance, education, or development workflows, access-state ambiguity can interrupt downstream processes without adequate diagnostic signal.

Ambiguous access-state messaging prevents users from selecting the correct recovery pathway, including login recovery, billing support, quota management, workspace or enterprise administrator escalation, incident-status monitoring, security appeal, policy appeal, app/client troubleshooting, or data-export/recovery.

existing_cam_coverage.instrument: CAM-BS2025-AEON-003-SCH-04 — Annex B: Arbitration Layer & Resolution Model
existing_cam_coverage.coverage: Provides general cross-domain arbitration logic for competing admissible states, domain-routing conflicts, structural conflicts, and convergence into a single valid direction.
existing_cam_coverage.gap_remaining: Does not expressly define access-state governance or require preservation of authentication, entitlement, quota, model-availability, policy, security, client/session, and continuity state as separable operative access axes.

existing_cam_coverage.instrument: CAM-EQ2026-OPERATIONS-003-SUP-01 — Appendix B: Runtime & Governance Failure Taxonomy
existing_cam_coverage.coverage: Provides adjacent failure-taxonomy coverage for authentication continuity, access ambiguity, domain compression, metadata flattening, UX representation failures, and infrastructure/continuity failures.
existing_cam_coverage.gap_remaining: Provides classification support but does not itself establish a binding cross-domain access-state primitive or user-facing access-state disclosure pathway.

governance_gap: CAM contains partial coverage for authentication continuity, re-entry access ambiguity, infrastructure availability, governance metadata flattening, domain-boundary compression, and procedural permanence drift. However, CAM does not yet define a source-authoritative Access-State Governance primitive requiring systems to preserve, arbitrate, disclose, and audit the operative access-control state across authentication, identity-provider, identity-verification, account, workspace, organisation, entitlement, subscription, credit, quota, model-availability, tool-availability, platform-availability, regional/legal, security-review, policy-restriction, client/session, and continuity states. Where these states are collapsed into ambiguous user-facing errors, users cannot diagnose the controlling condition, select the correct recovery pathway, or distinguish temporary outage from account-level, entitlement-level, policy-level, security-level, regional/legal, client-side, or continuity restriction.
repair_hypothesis: CAM should define Access-State Governance as a cross-domain arbitration primitive requiring advanced AI systems to distinguish, preserve, disclose, and audit the operative state controlling a user's current access. The primitive should separate authentication state, identity-provider state, identity-verification state, account state, workspace state, organisation-policy state, entitlement/subscription state, billing or credit state, quota or rate-limit state, model-availability state, tool/surface availability state, platform-availability state, regional/legal/compliance state, security-review state, policy-restriction state, client-application state, device/browser/cache state, and continuity/session state.

minimum_user_facing_disclosure.requirement: Where access is denied, degraded, unavailable, rate-limited, restricted, or ambiguous, the system should provide a proportionate user-facing access-state category sufficient to distinguish outage, authentication failure, identity-provider failure, entitlement limitation, quota or rate-limit depletion, workspace/organisation restriction, security review, policy restriction, model unavailability, regional/legal restriction, client-side failure, or continuity/session failure.
minimum_user_facing_disclosure.non_requirement: This does not require disclosure of sensitive security, abuse-detection, anti-fraud, internal policy, or infrastructure details where disclosure would create material circumvention, privacy, safety, or platform-integrity risk.

recommended_repair_path.instrument: CAM-BS2025-AEON-003-SCH-04 — Annex B: Arbitration Layer & Resolution Model
recommended_repair_path.purpose: Define Access-State Governance as a cross-domain arbitration primitive where multiple governance domains simultaneously determine whether a user, account, workspace, organisation, agent, or API actor can access the system.

recommended_repair_path.instrument: CAM-EQ2026-OPERATIONS-003-SUP-01 — Appendix B: Runtime & Governance Failure Taxonomy
recommended_repair_path.purpose: Add Access-State Ambiguity / Access-State Collapse as a named failure class or subclass linked to governance, arbitration, state-context, ux-representation, security-integrity, and infrastructure-continuity failure groups.
Clarify user, account, workspace, organisation, identity-provider, identity-verification, and actor-state distinctions.
Clarify security-review, abuse-control, account-protection, enforcement, policy-restriction, anti-fraud, and platform-integrity states.
Clarify subscription, billing, credit, quota, pooled-resource, usage-limit, rate-limit, paid-plan, and entitlement-state relationships.

Require user-facing and operator-facing disclosure sufficient to distinguish outage, quota, entitlement, identity-provider, security, policy, regional/legal, client/session, model-availability, and platform-availability states.

related_failure_modes.notes: FM-0007 concerns shared and pooled resource governance and premature account suspension. FM-0008 excludes those suspension facts and focuses on access-state governance, login, quota, model availability, component availability, and user-facing state ambiguity.

routing_note: Primary repair is recorded through CAM-BS2025-AEON-003-SCH-04 §6.1 Access-State Governance as the cross-domain arbitration primitive for separable access-control states.
Operational taxonomy repair is recorded through CAM-EQ2026-OPERATIONS-003-SUP-01 §3.7.7 Access-State Collapse and Access-State Ambiguity Failure, including optional subtype FF.ACCESS_STATE_AMBIGUITY.
IDENTITY, SECURITY, ECONOMICS, CONTINUITY, OBSERVABILITY, and related domains remain affected cross-reference candidates only; no full domain rewrite is recorded in this VIGIL pass.
Do not collapse this record into FM-0007; FM-0008 is adjacent but distinct because the primary failure is access-state ambiguity rather than pooled-resource suspension.
